mnemonics: It's a Package Deal: Think of returning an object like returning a complete, pre-packed lunchbox (the object) instead of listing each item separately (its individual variables).
keyPoints: A method can return an object of any class type, not just primitives.
The returned object can be a new instance or an existing one (e.g., from the parameters).
This allows for method chaining (e.g., builder().setName().build()).
commonMistakes: Forgetting that the returned object is a reference, so modifying it will modify the original if it wasn't a new instance.
Returning null instead of a valid object, leading to NullPointerExceptions.
Not assigning the returned object to a variable, causing it to be lost unless used immediately in an expression.
